questions.question: What is a tissue?
questions.correct_answer: A group of cells similar in structure and function working together to perform a specific task
questions.explanation: Tissues are formed when similar cells group together to perform specialized functions in organisms.

questions.question: What are the two main types of plant tissues?
questions.explanation: Plant tissues are classified based on their ability to divide and their functions.

questions.question: What is meristematic tissue?
questions.correct_answer: Plant tissue that actively divides and helps in plant growth
questions.explanation: Meristematic cells continuously divide and are found in growing regions of plants.

questions.question: Name the three types of meristematic tissues.
questions.explanation: These types differ based on their location in the plant body.

questions.question: What are permanent tissues?
questions.correct_answer: Tissues formed when meristematic cells lose the ability to divide
questions.explanation: Permanent tissues perform specific functions such as storage, support, and transport.

questions.question: Name the three types of simple permanent tissues.
questions.explanation: These tissues are composed of similar cells performing specific functions.

questions.question: Which plant tissue transports water and minerals?
questions.explanation: Xylem transports water and dissolved minerals from roots to other parts of the plant.

questions.question: Which plant tissue transports food?
questions.explanation: Phloem transports food produced in leaves to other parts of the plant.

questions.question: Name the four main types of animal tissues.
questions.correct_answer: Epithelial, connective, muscular and nervous tissues
questions.explanation: Animal tissues are classified based on their functions in the body.

questions.question: Which tissue is responsible for movement in the body?
questions.explanation: Muscular tissues contract and relax to cause movement.

notes.title: Tissue Definition
notes.content: A tissue is a group of similar cells that work together to perform a specific function in living organisms.

notes.title: Plant Tissue Types
notes.content: Plant tissues are classified into meristematic tissues and permanent tissues based on their ability to divide.

notes.title: Simple Permanent Tissues
notes.content: Parenchyma, collenchyma and sclerenchyma are simple tissues that perform functions such as storage, support and flexibility.

notes.title: Complex Permanent Tissues
notes.content: Xylem and phloem are complex tissues responsible for transporting water, minerals and food in plants.

notes.title: Animal Tissue Types
notes.content: Animal tissues include epithelial, connective, muscular and nervous tissues which perform protection, support, movement and coordination.

facts.fact: Meristematic tissues are responsible for plant growth and are found at the tips of roots and stems.
Parenchyma cells are living cells that often store food and may contain chlorophyll.
Xylem transports water while phloem transports food in plants.
Neurons are specialized cells that transmit nerve impulses throughout the body.
